title: Lotus tenuifolius
taxonomy: Lotus tenuifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, but it is sometimes placed in the family Leguminosae. It is native to Australia and is considered a rare and endangered species. The classification of this species is not universally agreed upon, with different sources listing it under various family names. However, it is widely accepted as a member of the genus Lotus. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate classification of Lotus tenuifolius.

Some research suggests that Lotus tenuifolius may have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects.
